title: Walking Skeleton, Core Behavior, and Preserved Invariants · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for walking skeleton, core behavior, and preserved invariants and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for cumulative working product with invariant evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.
Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.
Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Refactor Evidence symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Refactor Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Refactor Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior.

stimulus.title: Refactor Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Walking Skeleton
steps.instruction:
Order the Walking Skeleton evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Refactor Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
